title: Boeta's Leg of Lamb
excerpt: Ingredients 4 large onions, cut into thick slices 8 (or as many as you like) large cloves of garlic, roughly chopped 8 large sprigs of thyme 6 large sprigs of rosemary 20 ml dried oregano 8 thick slices of lemon, peel and white pith removed 1 nice large deboned leg of lamb, cut open and flattened salt and coarse black pepper …

seo.title: Boeta's Leg of Lamb - Marinda Kook

html:
Ingredients

  • 4 large onions, cut into thick slices
  • 8 (or as many as you like) large cloves of garlic, roughly chopped
  • 8 large sprigs of thyme
  • 6 large sprigs of rosemary
  • 20 ml dried oregano
  • 8 thick slices of lemon, peel and white pith removed
  • 1 nice large deboned leg of lamb, cut open and flattened
  • salt and coarse black pepper to taste
  • 250 ml red wine
  • 250 ml lamb stock (you can also use beef stock)
  • 250 ml Greek yoghurt
  • 125 ml olive oil
  • to serve: sprigs of rosemary and mint

Method

Mix the onions, garlic, thyme, rosemary, oregano and lemon together. Place half of the mixture in the bottom of a large roasting bag which you place inside an ovenproof dish.

Season the leg of lamb with salt and pepper and place on top of the onion mixture in the bag. Pack the rest of the onion mixture on top of the meat.

Mix the wine, stock, yoghurt and oil together and pour over the meat. Tie the bag closed (leave a little room for the heat to expand). This can marinate overnight in the fridge if you have time.

To cook, poke a small hole in the roasting bag and bake at 190ºC for an hour. Then lower the heat to 180ºC and bake further until meltingly tender, for about 2½ hours.

Carefully open the roasting bag and place the leg on a nice serving platter. Cover with foil and keep warm. Pour the juices from the bag through a sieve into a saucepan. Skim off the excess fat and boil the sauce rapidly, uncovered, until it reduces and thickens to your liking.

Cut or break the meat into portions. Arrange it on top of mashed potatoes, pour the sauce over and garnish with fresh herbs.

Serves 6 – 8 portions with pita breads, Tzatziki (Greek cucumber sauce) and Greek salad.

Easy!
